meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Finish Projects & Drive Revenue
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your MVP. Use this intermediate guide to implement SaaS pricing, leverage Claude Code, and finally launch your product to paying users.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of market validation. By implementing a concrete SaaS pricing model early, you force yourself to define value. Use Antigravity to scaffold your billing infrastructure and Claude Code to iterate rapidly, transforming your 'perfect' code into a revenue-generating asset today.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' codebases that never saw a single transaction. You aren't suffering from a lack of technical skill; you are suffering from a lack of stakes. When your project has no price tag, it has no urgency. The moment you define a subscription tier, the abstract 'project' becomes a business.

We are shifting your focus from endless feature creep to shipping a revenue-ready MVP. By integrating pricing logic into your architecture from Day 1, you align your development velocity with real-world market feedback. It’s time to stop building for the void and start building for the bottom line.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the unit of value your customer pays for. This prevents scope creep by aligning features with revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project scope and propose three SaaS pricing tiers based on a value-metric model. Define the features for each tier to ensure the MVP remains lean.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing.json schema defining my three tiers and their specific resource limits.'

steps.title: Architecting the Billing Gateway
steps.description: Integrate Stripe or LemonSqueezy hooks early. This makes your project 'real' and forces you to handle user authentication.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust backend service module for handling Stripe webhooks. Include logic to transition user subscription states based on payment success/failure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement stripe-webhook-handler.ts with error handling for failed payments.'

steps.title: Implementing Feature Gating
steps.description: Use your pricing model to toggle features. This forces you to build modular components that can be activated or deactivated instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware decorator that checks the user's subscription tier from the database before granting access to premium API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor all protected routes to use the new @requiresTier decorator.'

steps.title: Building the Paywall UI
steps.description: Create high-converting landing pages that display your pricing. This is the primary hurdle for most founders.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a responsive pricing component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the 'Pro' tier and includes a toggle for monthly versus yearly billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create PricingTable.tsx with responsive grid layout and annual savings badge.'

steps.title: Automated Deployment to Production
steps.description: Deploy to a live environment immediately. If you aren't deploying, you aren't building a SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline configuration that runs a smoke test on the billing integration before every production deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure github-actions pipeline to verify payment gateway connectivity.'

pro_tips: Never build an admin dashboard until you have your first 10 paying users; keep the backend simple.
Offer a 'Founders Edition' lifetime deal to your first 50 users to validate the product and secure initial capital.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests for the billing logic; never ship payment code without 100% coverage.
If a feature doesn't fit into a pricing tier, delete it. If it doesn't add value, it adds technical debt.
Focus on the 'Time to First Dollar' metric—if a feature increases this time, delay it until after launch.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment, run a live transaction in test mode, and then reach out to three potential users to validate your pricing tiers.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your project gather dust in a local repository while you chase feature perfection. Real founders ship, measure, and iterate. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
